name: the-cold-war-new-history
description: John Lewis Gaddis' 'The Cold War: A New History' — a concise, authoritative history of the Cold War from a Pulitzer Prize-winning historian, covering the ideological struggle that shaped the 20th century.
useCase: Understand the origins, key crises, nuclear strategy, major figures, and legacy of the Cold War.
knownRisks: Concise single volume — cannot cover every aspect in detail
Author has a specific interpretive framework — other historians disagree on some points
US/Western perspective — Soviet archives are not fully open
